title: PACS Image Transfer Failures
spl:
index=imaging sourcetype="dicom:dimse" sop_class="1.2.840.10008.5.1.4.1.1.2"
| where status!=0 OR move_status!="SUCCESS"
| stats count, values(study_uid) as studies by calling_ae, called_ae
| sort -count
description: Failed C-STORE or Q/R delays impact radiology reads and surgical planning.
value: Failed C-STORE or Q/R delays impact radiology reads and surgical planning.
grandmaExplanation: Failed C-STORE or Q/R delays impact radiology reads and surgical planning — so we know when protection is off, out of date, or bypassed.
implementation: Monitor association release and reject reasons; correlate with network MTU issues for large MR series.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: DICOM router / PACS logs.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="dicom:dimse"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Monitor association release and reject reasons; correlate with network MTU issues for large MR series.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=imaging sourcetype="dicom:dimse" sop_class="1.2.840.10008.5.1.4.1.1.2"
| where status!=0 OR move_status!="SUCCESS"
| stats count, values(study_uid) as studies by calling_ae, called_ae
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**PACS Image Transfer Failures** — Failed C-STORE or Q/R delays impact radiology reads and surgical planning.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="dicom:dimse"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): DICOM router / PACS logs. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: imaging; **sourcetype**: dicom:dimse. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=imaging, sourcetype="dicom:dimse". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where status!=0 OR move_status!="SUCCESS"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by calling_ae, called_ae** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (failing AE pairs), Line chart (failures over time), Bar chart (modalities).
visualization: Table (failing AE pairs), Line chart (failures over time), Bar chart (modalities).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
